sourceMovie.title: Hitman: Agent 47
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Hitman: Agent 47 for the same reason it broke through in 2015: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a crime rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Aleksander Bach keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The action and crime blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: John Wick: Chapter 4
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Hitman: Agent 47 clicked for you, John Wick: Chapter 4 (2023) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and crime, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Hitman: Agent 47?
faq.answer: Try John Wick: Chapter 4, Wanted, Run All Night—each shares DNA with Hitman: Agent 47 while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Hitman: Agent 47?
faq.answer: Hitman: Agent 47 (2015) is best described as Action, Crime, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Hitman: Agent 47 part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Hitman Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
